description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn with a dark patina and gold accent lines. The horn is mounted on a sturdy, rectangular wooden base with decorative paneling and a visible winding handle on one side. Atop the base sits a circular black turntable platter, holding a red-labeled vinyl record, with a metallic tonearm extending over it. The front of the base has a small metallic latch and a plaque, while the overall construction suggests a blend of wood, metal, and possibly some internal mechanical components. The design is ornate and classic, evoking early 20th-century aesthetics. This object would be a striking decorative or functional piece in an urban vintage shop, park installation, or themed public space.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and visible winding handle, featuring ornate details and a classic turntable platter.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the brass horn, the wooden base, and the winding handle on the left.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open mouth of the horn, the turntable platter, and the winding handle extending to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn's profile, the turntable platter, and the base's paneling visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view into the horn, revealing its internal ribbed structure and decorative rim, with the base and platter partially visible below.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and circular, the turntable platter is black with a red-labeled vinyl record on top. It is moderately sized compared to the base, occupying the central upper surface. The platter appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and a flat profile.
attribute_tonearm: Present, the tonearm is metallic, curved, and extends from the right side of the base over the platter. It is medium in length, with a jointed appearance, and has a dark, possibly black or silver finish. The tonearm is positioned as if ready to play the record.
